beast_pools.trs: Redlining emerged in the 1930s as a result of the Home Owners' Loan Corporation (HOLC) mapping system. These maps assessed mortgage risk, categorizing neighborhoods based on perceived investment security. Areas deemed 'risky' – often, but not always, those with large minority populations – were outlined in red, hence the term 'redlining'. This wasn’t necessarily about race; it was about financial risk. Banks simply didn't want to lend in areas where property values were expected to decline, and these areas happened to coincide with minority neighborhoods due to other economic factors
